sections.title: There is
sections.content:
We use **There is** for one thing:

• **There is** a book on the table.
• **There is** a cat in the garden.
• **There is** an apple in the bag.

sections.title: There are
sections.content:
We use **There are** for more than one:

• **There are** three books on the table.
• **There are** two cats in the garden.
• **There are** many people here.

sections.title: Questions and Negatives
sections.content:
**Question:** **Is there** a book? **Are there** any books?

**Negative:** **There is not** (isn't) a book. **There are not** (aren't) any books.

sections.title: Past: There was / There were
sections.content:
For the past:

• **There was** a party yesterday.
• **There were** many people at the party.

questions.question: _____ three chairs in the room.
questions.explanation: Three chairs = more than one. We use There are.

questions.question: _____ any milk in the fridge?
questions.explanation: Milk is uncountable. Question: Is there.

questions.question: _____ a shop near here?
questions.explanation: Question about one thing: Is there.
